title: Level 172

Level 172

{{Underline|242424}}
"Memorium"

{{Audio|file=Memorium_Ambience.mp3|volume=0.5|align=center}}
{{Class 0|class=Iota|header=Threat Index:|safety=Highly Unpredictable|security=Cognitively Hazardous|entity=Temporally Distorted|hexagon=ι|color-h=60|color-s=1%|color-l=27%}} {{Underline|242424}}
[[File:B-rooms2.jpg|center|455x455px|link=]]

''“Everything inside looks abandoned. It's in complete ruin, yet I can't help but feel that it was never occupied to begin with...”''
==
'''Description'''
== {{Underline|242424}} {{Simple Image Block|image=B-rooms.jpg|caption=The first known photograph ever taken of ''Level 172'', dated July 3, 1998.|width=300px}}
''Level 172'', or, as it is more colloquially known, ''"Memorium"'', consists of a large, desolate funeral home approximately 17,000 square feet in area, and is prone to structural failure, although any alterations in the level's structure are reverted while unobserved. Accurate documentation of the level's layout has not yet been feasible due to the various anomalous properties ''Level 172'' possesses, and the difficulty of entry into said level. ''Level 172'' is devoid of any form of sustenance or potable water, though some wanderers report seeing old bottles of ''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]'' within some of the caskets that litter the floors of the level. As mentioned, the level contains numerous caskets and other objects customary to a funeral home, though said objects are always strewn about haphazardly and can clutter much of the level's walking space. Several deceased wanderers have been found within the caskets of ''Level 172''; despite this, all of said wanderers are alive and well outside of the level. It is believed that true death is impossible within ''Level 172'', when wanderers die within the level, their memories of the level become fragmented, and they are randomly transported to one of several specific levels with an intense headache. This transportation is instantaneous; wanderers who die within the level may reappear outside it mere seconds after entering, despite subjectively spending hours, days, or even years inside before dying. Nearly all levels that wanderers are transported to can be reached naturally through exits in ''Level 172'', aside from a select few, though it is theorized that ''Level 172'' contains exits to those levels as well, and they have simply not been found yet. The phenomenon appears to apply to ''[[Facelings]]'' as well, though no living specimens have been spotted in the level; only the corpses of said ''[[Facelings]]'' within caskets. ''Level 172'' is well known for its unpredictability and its tendency to change depending on the memories and experiences of wanderers who visit it, causing information about the level to, on occasion, become partially inaccurate. These anomalous properties can make ''Level 172'' dangerous and confusing, and often cause wanderers to get lost within.
===
'''Anomalous Properties'''
=== {{Underline|242424}} {{Simple Image Block|image=Memorium.png|caption=A photo of ''Level 172'' taken on April 7, 2007. The image appears unnaturally desaturated.|width=300px}}
''Level 172'' harbors numerous anomalous properties that range vastly in effects. For instance, any and all electrical equipment used to record or photograph the level produces seemingly random results when used; photos are replaced by images of random places on Earth, video recordings behave similarly, however, they are often unstable, resulting in nonsensical compositions of various unrelated footage. The level can still be photographed using non-electrical cameras, but cannot be filmed, and any photos taken often appear desaturated. This desaturation also appears to affect the memories of wanderers, as wanderers state that their memories match the photos they take, despite photos from other wanderers contradicting them. Along with electrical devices, temporal consistency within ''Level 172'' also behaves improperly, as the amount of time elapsed for the wanderer within the level can differ drastically from time elapsed in the rest of ''[[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]]'', and some wanderers have reportedly exited during a time period before their initial entry. Despite this violation of causality, no duplicate wanderers have ever been found, despite the thorough searching that ''[[M.E.G.]]'' has conducted. Wanderers typically experience intense headaches after exiting ''Level 172''; these headaches are usually far more intense if the wanderer had died within the level. As stated previously, wanderers who find themselves outside of ''Level 172'' after dying inside it have fragmented memories, causing many to believe that what had occurred was a dream. Any wanderer who has previously died within ''Level 172'' is incapable of entering it again, as they will instantly pass out and be transported to one of the levels that ''Level 172'' exits into. Doing this causes extraordinarily intense migraines and, at times, vomiting.
==
'''Entities'''
== {{Underline|242424}}
Entities were thought to be absent from ''Level 172'' for several years after the initial discovery of the level, but recent information has led many to believe otherwise. Very little is known about the entities, colloquially known as ''"Blanks"''; however, they are presumed to be non-hostile, as no reports of violent encounters have been presented. These entities have been rumored to exist since 2001, possibly before then, but no photographic evidence was documented until October 17, 2018. Many theorize that ''"Blanks"'' are a form of mutated ''[[Facelings|Faceling]]''. Witness accounts often describe ''"Blanks"'' as unnaturally tall, frail, pallid humanoid figures that avoid human contact and vacate any area occupied by them. Some wanderers have stated that ''"Blanks"'' often stalk them from a distance, but when noticed, immediately retreat. One wanderer reported seeing a ''"Blank"'' carrying a human corpse and depositing it into a casket, leading many to theorize that the entities exist to maintain the level. Aside from the elusive ''"Blanks"'', no other entities are known to inhabit ''Level 172'', and the level is considered generally safe.
[[File:ScaryMemorium.jpg|center|455x455px|link=]]
A photograph of an unspecified room within ''Level 172''. The photographer is seemingly oblivious to the entity observing them.
==
'''Colonies and Outposts'''
== {{Underline|242424}}
Due to the nature of ''Level 172'', colonies and outposts are considered impractical to establish or maintain. While occasional signs of habitation have been observed throughout the level, most believe these are born of necessity rather than deliberate settlement, as egress from ''Level 172'' can be difficult. As a result, some wanderers may construct temporary shelters while attempting to locate an exit.
==
'''Entrances and Exits'''
== {{Underline|242424}} ===
'''Entrances'''
=== {{Underline|242424}}
Entry into ''Level 172'' is seldom consistent and not well documented, and only a few methods of entry are confirmed. * Noclipping into a tree with no bark on ''[[Level 182]]'' will lead here. * Caskets can be very rarely found on ''[[Level 110]]'', and sleeping in them may take you here. * Wanderers report entering ''Level 172'' from ''[[Level 141]]'', though no confirmed method has yet been found. ===
'''Exits'''
=== {{Underline|242424}} Several conventional exits from ''Level 172'' are theorized to exist, but have yet to be discovered, as dying within this level, or entering this level after having previously died in it, can lead to a variety of different levels, many of which have confirmed natural ways of exiting into. * Lying down and turning a casket upside down onto oneself may lead to ''[[Level 5]]''. * Any water found within the level, whether it be bottled or spilled, can be used in some way to reach ''[[Level 191]]'', though the exact method is unclear. * It is thought that directly and intentionally touching any flora on ''Level 172'', whether it be moss, potted plants, or otherwise, can lead to ''[[Level 317]]'', as doing so is the last thing many wanderers remember before appearing on said level. * Harming oneself on ''Level 172'' can cause a temporary coma, and instant transportation to ''[[Level 16]]''. Below are the levels that can be accessed through death within ''Level 172'', or entry into the level after having previously died within, but for which no conventional exits have been discovered. It is to be noted that the destination is predominantly random and does not seem to have any pattern, though some speculate that the specific means of death may make a certain destination more common. * ''[[Level 798]]'' (Starvation is theorized to increase the likelihood of arrival at ''[[Level 798]]'', though this is not confirmed.) * ''[[Level 908]]'' (Unconfirmed) * ''[[Level 305]]'' * ''[[Level 144]]''
